paragraphs.text: The proposal of the Declaration on Digital Rights and Principles of the European Union puts the individual at the heart of the digital transformation, committing to ensuring inclusive, fair, safe and sustainable access to the digital environment. This initiative is particularly relevant because it addresses the relationship between consumers and digital platforms not only as taxable users but as subjects who have rights in the digital space. Therefore, a future Digital Fair Act building on these principles could strengthen the protection of digital consumers against platform abuse, ensuring more equal conditions for access to and use of digital services.
